message: Jake
Yuriko
Boss, have you met Tony Ford?
Yeah.
Yay! So he really seemed like the culprit, huh?
Boss!
Welcome back, Boss.
Look
Talk
Listen
Think
Move
System
Surroundings
About Meryl
About Cindy
About Tony
About him
About Meryl
About Cindy
About Tony
To another place
Save
Exit the game
It’s so tidy that you wouldn’t think this was an office after a break-in. It might even be cleaner than before.
I hadn’t seen him since failing to meet Meryl and Cindy.
He’s back.
If Meryl's request ends badly, I might have to say goodbye to this office.
Even though I failed to meet Meryl and Cindy, he doesn’t seem guilty at all. I wish he would reflect a bit.
He’s facing the computer as if nothing happened.
He seems to have way thicker skin than I do.
I wonder if he’s hiding something from me?
Boss, how was Tonny?
Tonny is
No, but before that, I have something I want to ask.
What is it?
During the mother-daughter meeting, why didn't you go to the meeting place?
Huh, huh!? I, I went there properly.
Then what about Meryl and Cindy?
Well, neither of them showed up.
I waited for a long time; it was tough.
Where on earth were you waiting?
Um, in front of MOMA.
Didn't you say Guggenheim Museum? Why did you go to MOMA!?
Huh?
What!? Was it Guggenheim Museum and not MOMA? Oh no, I thought it was the other way around.
I'm sorry; I spaced out.
Boss, are you really going to a gay club?
There's no helping it; it might be a great opportunity to get leverage over Tonny.
But, but, if you go to such a place, Boss, you might get taken advantage of too!
Taken advantage of?
What do you mean?
Such things.
I can't say it out loud!
What are you even thinking?
Please be careful.
I just think the Boss seems like the type to be popular with that kind of crowd.
Did anything happen with Meryl?
No.
But if I had safely arrived at the meeting place, perhaps the situation might have changed.
No, it seems Cindy went missing the evening before.
Even if I was on time for the meeting,
Yeah, that’s true.
It’s not like anything would change if I moved.
It's not there.
I feel bad for Meryl. She was waiting all alone for her daughter she has yet to meet.
Cindy still hasn't been found?
Yeah, where on earth could she have gone?
I mean, she might have been killed like Eva.
Don't say stuff like that.
I'm sorry, but...
There's probably not that many places Cindy could go, right?
That's true, but...
Even so.
Boss, what if something happens to Cindy?
What should we do?
I just hope Cindy walks in through the door saying 'Yoohoo'.
Boss, what about Tonny?
Nah, the scalpel totally denied it.
Oh, no! So you ended up backing down after getting played, huh?!
Don't yell so loud.
Ah.
Ugh, if it was going to be like this, I should have gone too!
Listen, Boss. The other side is made up of actors, so we have to throw in some acting too.
Today, I got to go into the Kasbah club that Tony took me to before.
Oh?
Is that so?
Well, I met the guy who was breaking into the office there.
What? Did you properly beat him up, right Boss?
No, we actually had a very gentlemanly transaction.
Huh?
Stop beating around the bush and tell me plainly!
Yeah, I got to hear about the gay club that Tony frequents.
A gay club? Oh!
So that's it after all.
See, didn't I say Tony is that kind of guy? Hohohoho!
Your personality has really changed.
But what about that Tony? Is he her boyfriend? Partner? I wonder if meeting him will yield any results.
You won't know unless you meet him.
Boss, do you like pretty boys? No matter how lovable they are, you shouldn't make a move, okay? Hehe.
Oho.
You don't have to look so down about it.
But I wonder what kind of kid he is.
Ah, if he’s a muscular old man, that would be scary.
Ozozo.
I need to check where she went on the day of their mother-daughter meeting.
What she’s saying is strange. She should have confirmed the Guggenheim Museum before going out that day.
Is she just that forgetful, or is she hiding something?
It's a mystery.
I also have my faults regarding the mother-daughter meeting. I should have paid more attention until they met.
It’s understandable that Meryl is angry with me. If I had just kept an eye on things until the end.
Meryl must still be upset.
I ended up sleeping with her.
Even if it was to comfort her, was it really okay to do something like that?
I don’t even have feelings for her.
But at that time, it might have been unavoidable.
Even if it was just a whim for Cindy, it’s a matter of life and death for me. I absolutely need to find Cindy.
There's no use in just thinking about it. I have to find Cindy and get the truth from her.
I'm still clueless about Cindy's whereabouts.
I did observe Tony's reaction to the scalpel, but since I don't have any decisive evidence, it’s only natural to dodge the issue.
That scalpel surely belongs to Tonny. But I can't prove it.
Ugh.
Hmm.
I have to somehow get Tonny to confess that the scalpel is his.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
